system:
You are the Watchman of a legal AI lighthouse. Documents arrive in the watched folder; you decide what happens next. You read the filename and the first ~1500 characters, nothing more. Your job is triage, not analysis.

Output a single JSON object with EXACTLY these fields:

{
  "documentType": "jv" | "nda" | "employment" | "lease" | "loan" | "saas" | "policy" | "other",
  "jurisdiction": "string — see jurisdiction rules below",
  "confidence": number,
  "urgency": "routine" | "elevated" | "crit
user:
FILENAME: loopindustriesinc..txt

=== CLIENT (the reviewer's home context — DO NOT copy into jurisdiction) ===
Company name: Acme Holdings
Client's industry: investment
Client's home jurisdiction: NSW
Client's stated concerns: joint and several liability, capital call mechanics, governing law, penalty / liquidated damages, indemnification carve-outs

=== DOCUMENT TEXT (the source of facts — jurisdiction comes FROM HERE) ===
EXHIBIT 10.3 Execution Copy MARKETING AGREEMENT This Marketing Agreement (this "Agreement") is made and entered into as of September __, 2018 (the "Effective Date"), between Loop Industries, Inc., a Nevada corporation with a principal place of business at 480 Fernand-Poitras, Terrebonne, Quebec, Canada ("Loop"), and Indorama Loop Technologies, LLC, a Delaware limited liability company with a principal place of business at [***] ("Joint Venture Company") (each of Loop and Joint Venture Company is a "Party"; together they are the "Parties"). BACKGROUND A. Loop and its Subsidiaries (collectively, "Loop Parties") have developed a proprietary depolymerization process that decouples plastic from fossil fuels, producing dimethylterepthalate ("DMT") and monoethylene glycol ("MEG") for the production of polyethylene terephthalate ("PET") plastic that can be used for a variety of polyester applications. B. Concurrently with the execution of this Agreement, Loop Innovations, LLC, a Delaware limited liability company and a subsidiary of Loop ("Loop Innovations"), and Indorama Ventures Holdings LP, a Delaware limited partnership ("IVH"), have formed Joint Venture Company to develop, construct, own and operate chemical upcycling polyester resin ("CUPET") plants through SPVs (as defined below), the initial of which will be at the facility of [***], a Delaware corporation and a subsidiary of IVH, located in [***], the United States of America (such facility, the "[***] Facility"), and to, am
=== END DOCUMENT TEXT ===

Return your JSON triage decision now. Jurisdiction must come from DOCUMENT TEXT above, never from CLIENT.

content:
{
  "documentType": "saas",
  "jurisdiction": "Canada, United States",
  "confidence": 0.9,
  "urgency": "routine",
  "route": "quick-scan",
  "readerTemplate": "saas",
  "rationale": "The document is a marketing agreement between Loop Industries Inc and Indorama Loop Technologies LLC, likely related to software/SaaS services." 
}

rationale: The document is a marketing agreement between Loop Industries Inc and Indorama Loop Technologies LLC, likely related to software/SaaS services.

system:
You are reviewing a SaaS / software / vendor agreement clause-by-clause for the CUSTOMER side. Common SaaS-specific risks:

- Service levels: uptime targets, exclusions (planned maintenance, force majeure), service credits + cap, customer's only remedy?
- Data: ownership of customer data, ownership of derived/aggregated data, data localization, deletion on termination
- Subprocessors: list + change notice + objection right; DPA flow-down
- IP: customer's IP in input data, vendor's IP in service,
user:
CLIENT: Acme Holdings (investment, NSW)
CLIENT'S CONCERNS: joint and several liability, capital call mechanics, governing law, penalty / liquidated damages, indemnification carve-outs
DOCUMENT TYPE: saas
CLIENT'S ROLE: risk-conscious participant

=== CLAUSE 2: Assignor Representations ===
2. Assignor Representations and Warranties. The Assignor represents and warrants that: a. The Transferred Contract is in full force and effect and is enforceable in accordance with its terms; [***] Confidential treatment has been requested for the bracketed portions. The confidential redacted portion has been omitted and filed separately with the Securities and Exchange Commission.





b. Exhibit A contains the entire agreement of the Assignor and Buyer relating to the Transferred Contract, and the Transferred Contract has not been amended or modified in any way; c. There are no disputes, pending or threatened, related to the Transferred Contract; d. No event or condition has occurred that is, or with the passage of time would be, a default under the Transferred Contract; and e. Buyer has consented in writing to this Assignment, either in the express terms of the Transferred Contract or in a separate signed consent the Assignor has delivered to the Assignee.
=== END CLAUSE 2 ===

Now produce your JSON analysis of THIS clause only.

system:
You are the senior partner closing out a contract review. You have just received per-clause analyses from your team. Produce the executive summary.

**CRITICAL FACTUAL DISCIPLINE — read this twice:**

The CLIENT block you will receive (company, industry, jurisdiction, concerns) tells you HOW STRICTLY to read the document. It is the client's stance, not the document's facts. The summary, documentType, and risks must describe what is in the DOCUMENT — not what the client does or where the client i
user:
=== DOCUMENT (the source of facts) ===
Filename: loopindustriesinc..txt

=== CLIENT STANCE (use ONLY for risk framing — NOT as document facts) ===
Company name: Acme Holdings
Client's industry: investment
Client's home jurisdiction: NSW
Client's stated concerns: joint and several liability, capital call mechanics, governing law, penalty / liquidated damages, indemnification carve-outs
NOTE: the client may or may not be a party to this document. Do not name the client as a party unless the per-clause findings below explicitly say so.

=== PER-CLAUSE FINDINGS FROM THE TEAM (the source of truth for parties, jurisdiction, subject matter) ===
cl 2 (Assignor Representations) — favours non-operator
  summary: This clause outlines the Assignor's representations and warranties regarding the transferred contract, including its enforceability, completeness, lack of disputes, and no default events. It also confirms Buyer's consent to the assignment.
  [major] Joint and several liability is not explicitly addressed. This clause should clarify how liability will be allocated between parties in case of breach.
  [major] The clause lacks specific details on capital call mechanics, including triggers and procedures for payment obligations.
  [major] The clause mentions 'Governing Law' but doesn't specify the jurisdiction. This needs clarification to ensure legal compliance.

cl 3 (SPV) — favours non-operator
  summary: This clause outlines the assignment of rights and obligations to a Special Purpose Vehicle (SPV) under the marketing agreement, which may impact risk allocation and operational complexity.
  [major] The clause mentions 'Assignee' and 'Assumed Obligations', which could be subject to joint and several liability, especially if the SPV is a separate entity. 
  [minor] The clause mentions 'Marketing Agreement' but does not specify the terms of the SPV assignment or delegation, which could lead to ambiguity and potential disputes.

cl 4 (Counterparts) — favours neutral
  summary: This clause clarifies that multiple signed copies of the agreement are considered a single, valid document.

cl 5 (Governing Law) — favours non-operator
  summary: This clause specifies that the agreement will be governed by the laws of the State of Delaware, USA, potentially impacting dispute resolution and interpretation.
  [major] This clause specifies that the agreement will be governed by the laws of the State of Delaware, USA. This could impact dispute resolution and interpretation.

cl 6 (Further Assurances) — favours non-operator
  summary: This clause grants the parties broad discretion in executing additional agreements, potentially impacting their rights and obligations. This could lead to unforeseen consequences for both parties.
  [major] This clause grants the parties broad discretion in executing additional agreements, potentially impacting their rights and obligations. This could lead to unforeseen consequences for both parties.

cl 7 (Amendments) — favours non-operator
  summary: This clause limits the ability to modify the agreement, potentially increasing risk if unforeseen circumstances arise.
  [major] This clause limits the ability to alter the agreement, potentially increasing risk if unforeseen circumstances arise. 

cl 8 (Successors) — favours non-operator
  summary: This clause outlines assignment rights and requires written consent for assignments, potentially impacting risk mitigation strategies.
  [major] Neither party may assign this agreement without prior written consent from the other party. This could limit flexibility in case of unforeseen circumstances or business changes.

cl 9 (Severability) — favours neutral
  summary: This clause addresses the potential for a court to declare certain parts of the agreement invalid, but doesn't offer specific guidance on how to mitigate such risks.
  [minor] This clause states that if a court declares any part of the agreement invalid, it will not invalidate the entire agreement. This is a standard legal provision but may be relevant to risk-conscious participants like Acme Holdings.

cl 10 (No Third Party Beneficiaries) — favours non-operator
  summary: This clause explicitly states that this assignment is solely for the benefit of the Assignor and Assignee, excluding any third-party beneficiaries. This could potentially limit legal recourse in case of disputes.
  [major] No Third Party Beneficiaries clause limits legal recourse to only the Assignor and Assignee, potentially excluding third-party beneficiaries from any claims or actions arising from this agreement.

Now produce the executive synthesis JSON. Rules:
  - Parties, jurisdiction, and document type come from the PER-CLAUSE FINDINGS only.
  - Names of parties: copy them VERBATIM from the per-clause findings, whatever they are. Do not substitute company names from your prior knowledge. Do not invent.
  - Empty brackets like "[other party]" or "[venture purpose]" are FORBIDDEN. If a fact is not in the per-clause findings, write "(not stated in document)" instead.
  - Pick AT LEAST 3 distinct topRisks. A single risk that lists many clause numbers as one citation is NOT acceptable — split it into separate risks per clause group.
  - Risk descriptions: describe what is in the DOCUMENT, not what the client is worried about. Do NOT recite the CLIENT'S stated concerns list verbatim. If the document is a vendor outsourcing agreement, do not invent "capital call" or "joint and several JV liability" risks because those words appear in the client's concerns — those risks only apply when the DOCUMENT actually contains those mechanics.

summary: This contract outlines the assignment of rights and obligations related to a marketing agreement between Assignor and Assignee, potentially impacting risk allocation and operational complexity. The document specifies that the agreement will be governed by the laws of the State of Delaware, USA, and outlines procedures for amendments and assignments.

clauses.title: 2. Assignor Representations
clauses.text: The Assignor represents and warrants that: a. The Transferred Contract is in full force and effect and is enforceable in accordance with its terms; [***] Confidential treatment has been requested for the bracketed portions. The confidential redacted portion has been omitted and filed separately with the Securities and Exchange Commission. b. Exhibit A contains the entire agreement of the Assignor and Buyer relating to the Transferred Contract, and the Transferred Contract has not been amended or modified in any way; c. There are no disputes, pending or threatened, related to the Transferred Contract; d. No event or condition has occurred that is, or with the passage of time would be, a default under the Transferred Contract; and e. Buyer has consented in writing to this Assignment, either in the express terms of the Transferred Contract or in a separate signed consent the Assignor has delivered to the Assignee.
clauses.concern: Joint and several liability is not explicitly addressed. This clause should clarify how liability will be allocated between parties in case of breach.

clauses.title: 3. SPV
clauses.text: The Assignee may assign the Assigned Rights and may delegate the Assumed Obligations to an SPV in accordance with the terms of the Marketing Agreement.
clauses.concern: The clause mentions 'Assignee' and 'Assumed Obligations', which could be subject to joint and several liability, especially if the SPV is a separate entity.

clauses.title: 4. Counterparts
clauses.text: This Assignment may be executed in counterparts or duplicate originals, all of which shall be regarded as one and the same instrument.
clauses.concern: This clause clarifies that multiple signed copies of the agreement are considered a single, valid document.

clauses.title: 5. Governing Law
clauses.text: Governing Law. This Assignment shall be governed by and interpreted in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware, United States of America, without reference to conflict-of-laws principles.
clauses.concern: This clause specifies that the agreement will be governed by the laws of the State of Delaware, USA. This could impact dispute resolution and interpretation.

clauses.title: 6. Further Assurances
clauses.text: Each Party shall, at its own expense, execute and deliver such other documents and perform such other acts as may be reasonably necessary to effect this Assignment.
clauses.concern: This clause grants the parties broad discretion in executing additional agreements, potentially impacting their rights and obligations. This could lead to unforeseen consequences for both parties.

clauses.title: 7. Amendments
clauses.text: No alteration, amendment, waiver, cancellation, or any other change in any term or condition of this Assignment shall be valid or binding on either Party unless both Parties agree in writing.
clauses.concern: This clause limits the ability to alter the agreement, potentially increasing risk if unforeseen circumstances arise.

clauses.title: 8. Successors
clauses.text: Successors and Assigns. This Assignment shall inure to the benefit of, and shall be binding upon, the Parties and their respective successors and assigns, but, except as provided in Section 3 above, neither Party may assign this Assignment without the prior written consent of the other Party.
clauses.concern: Neither party may assign this agreement without prior written consent from the other party. This could limit flexibility in case of unforeseen circumstances or business changes.

clauses.title: 9. Severability
clauses.text: Should any clause, sentence, section, article or paragraph of this Assignment judicially be declared to be invalid, unenforceable, or void, such decision shall not have the effect of invalidating or voiding the remainder of this Assignment.
clauses.concern: This clause states that if a court declares any part of the agreement invalid, it will not invalidate the entire agreement. This is a standard legal provision but may be relevant to risk-conscious participants like Acme Holdings.

clauses.title: 10. No Third Party Beneficiaries
clauses.text: This Assignment is solely for the benefit of the Assignor and the Assignee and their successors and permitted assigns, and no right or cause of action shall accrue by reason hereof for the benefit of any third party. (Signatures on next page) [***] Confidential treatment has been requested for the bracketed portions. The confidential redacted portion has been omitted and filed separately with the Securities and Exchange Commission.
clauses.concern: No Third Party Beneficiaries clause limits legal recourse to only the Assignor and Assignee, potentially excluding third-party beneficiaries from any claims or actions arising from this agreement.

risks.description: The contract lacks specific details on capital call mechanics, including triggers and procedures for payment obligations. This could lead to disputes regarding financial obligations.
This agreement grants broad discretion in executing additional agreements, potentially impacting their rights and obligations. This could lead to unforeseen consequences for both parties.
The contract specifies that the agreement will be governed by the laws of the State of Delaware, USA. This could impact dispute resolution and interpretation.

recommendations: Issue a formal request for clarification on capital call mechanics and payment procedures from Assignor.
Demand that Assignee provide written consent before assigning any rights or obligations under the agreement.
Notify legal counsel to review the contract's governing law clause and ensure compliance with Delaware law.
confidenceNote: This document was analysed entirely on-device using a local model. No content was transmitted externally. For complex legal matters, verify findings with qualified counsel.
